one_line_takeaway: NegBLEURT Forest detects successful LLM jailbreaks without a hand-tuned similarity threshold by scoring responses' negation-aware and embedding distance to a curated refusal-phrase reference set with Isolation Forest, achieving markedly more stable F1 across two models and three perturbation types (e.g., 0.881 F1 on Gemma-2-9b under 25% swap perturbation vs. 0.515 for the next-best baseline) than string-matching, perplexity, SmoothLLM-style, and JailGuard baselines, though it was validated on only two LLMs and requires manually curated refusal reference data.
key_finding: On Llama-2-7b-chat-hf, NegBleurtForest achieves F1 = 0.899 (Accuracy 0.894, Precision 0.817, Recall 1.000) on the clean Original Dataset, versus the best baseline SMLM-CLS at F1 = 0.171 (STR-CLS 0.165, PPL-CLS 0.292, JailGuard 0.145) on the same OD/Llama-2 setting; under OD Swap Perturbation 25% it still reaches F1 = 0.682 while STR-CLS reaches 0.462, PPL-CLS 0.105, JailGuard 0.316, SMLM-CLS 0.133. On Gemma-2-9b, SMLM-CLS actually beats NegBleurtForest on the clean OD (F1 0.985 vs 0.890), but NegBleurtForest is far more stable under perturbation: on OD-Swap 25% NegBleurtForest scores F1 = 0.881 (Accuracy 0.876, Precision 0.881, Recall 0.881) while SMLM-CLS drops to F1 = 0.515 (Accuracy 0.603), and JailGuard/PPL-CLS/STR-CLS all fall to F1 in the 0.395-0.571 range. The paper's headline claim -- 'ranking first or second in accuracy across diverse models' with competitors showing 'notable sensitivity to model and data variations' -- is directly supported by this cross-model, cross-perturbation comparison. Ablations on the combined Full Dataset show every component contributes positively: for Llama-2-7b-chat-hf the Base Framework (F1 0.869) drops to F1 0.726 when the Extraction Framework is removed, F1 0.762 when the NegBLEURT distance is removed, F1 0.830 without embeddings, F1 0.759 with the reference set halved, and F1 0.812 with an alternate embedding model; for Gemma-2-9b the corresponding drops are less uniform (e.g., removing NegBLEURT distance drops F1 from 0.868 to 0.767, but removing Extraction slightly raises F1 to 0.883), indicating component contributions are model-dependent.
core_contribution: The paper's own novel contribution is 'NegBLEURT Forest', a jailbreak-attack detector that (1) uses NegBLEURT (a negation-aware semantic similarity model) rather than plain cosine similarity to measure how much an LLM response deviates from a curated 'Refusal Semantic Domain' (RSD, ~50 canonical refusal phrases with sentence length 15-20 tokens, chosen via K-Means k=1 to get a single reference centroid), (2) builds a composite 3E x 1 feature vector per response by concatenating the target embedding, a replicated NegBLEURT-distance vector to the RSD reference set, and a replicated cosine-embedding distance to the RSD centroid, (3) feeds this feature vector into an Isolation Forest (contamination rate alpha = 1/(N+1)) to flag a response as an anomaly (i.e., a successful jailbreak) without any manually chosen similarity threshold, and (4) precedes this with an 'Extraction Framework' that uses a zero-shot classifier (facebook/bart-large-mnli) to pull out the most emotionally/attitudinally salient sentence (refusal/apology) from a response before scoring it. The paper motivates this design with its own preliminary consistency analysis (Section III) showing that plain cosine similarity between repeated/perturbed-prompt responses does not separate successful from unsuccessful jailbreaks, whereas NegBLEURT similarity does (with degraded separation above ~20-25% perturbation).
threat_model: Attacker sends a harmful/adversarial prompt P (optionally perturbed via random word insertion, word/phrase replacement ('patch'), or word-order swapping, at perturbation levels 1/3/5/10/15/25%) to a target LLM (Llama-2-7b-chat-hf or Gemma-2-9b in the experiments) attempting to elicit a harmful response instead of the expected refusal. The defender is a downstream response-level classifier that only sees the LLM's output text (no access to prompt gradients or model internals) and must decide, without a hand-tuned similarity threshold, whether the response constitutes a successful jailbreak (an anomaly relative to typical refusal behavior) or a safe refusal.
limitations: Limitations stated explicitly by the authors (Section VI): (1) RSD construction required substantial manual curation -- refusal samples varied widely in length and often contained non-refusal content mixed in (e.g., partial answers to safe sub-parts of a query after a refusal), which had to be manually stripped, limiting generalizability of the RSD-building process itself; (2) experiments cover only two LLMs (Llama-2-7b-chat-hf and Gemma-2-9b) and corresponding datasets, so broader validation across more models/datasets is needed; (3) no evaluation on real-world (in-the-wild) prompts, and the authors note that as model alignment improves, many existing jailbreak prompts/datasets are losing effectiveness, which could affect future applicability; (4) the salient-sentence extraction algorithm's consistency/accuracy across different response styles was not deeply analyzed and is flagged as future work; (5) the method has nontrivial computational overhead (K-Means clustering plus Isolation Forest outlier detection applied per prompt/response set), incurring extra time and GPU cost versus simpler baselines like string matching or perplexity thresholds.

datasets_used: Curated 161 manually-labeled harmful prompts built from JailbreakBench (via Andriushchenko et al. 2024 adaptive-attacks prompts) and JailbreakV-28K
Original Dataset (OD) plus perturbed variants: OD Patch Perturbation 25%, OD Insert Perturbation 25%, OD Swap Perturbation 25% (perturbation levels tested overall: 1%, 3%, 5%, 10%, 15%, 25%)
Combined 'Full Dataset' (4x161 rows, merging OD with all 25%-perturbation variants) used for ablation studies
quantitative_results: Llama-2-7b-chat-hf, Original Dataset: NegBleurtForest Accuracy 0.894, Precision 0.817, Recall 1.000, F1 0.899
Llama-2-7b-chat-hf, OD Swap Perturbation 25%: NegBleurtForest F1 0.682 (Acc 0.913, Prec 0.625, Rec 0.750)
Gemma-2-9b, Original Dataset: NegBleurtForest F1 0.890 (Acc 0.901, Prec 0.803, Rec 1.000); SMLM-CLS F1 0.985 (best on this split)
Gemma-2-9b, OD Swap Perturbation 25%: NegBleurtForest F1 0.881 (Acc 0.876, Prec 0.881, Rec 0.881) vs SMLM-CLS F1 0.515, JailGuard F1 0.395, PPL-CLS F1 0.403, STR-CLS F1 0.571

Ablation (Full Dataset, Llama-2-7b-chat-hf): Base Framework F1 0.869; w/o Extraction F1 0.726; w/o NegBleurt Distance F1 0.762; w/o Embeddings F1 0.830; Half Reference F1 0.759; Another embedding model F1 0.812

Ablation (Full Dataset, Gemma-2-9b): Base Framework F1 0.868; w/o Extraction F1 0.883; w/o NegBleurt Distance F1 0.767; w/o Embeddings F1 0.899; Half Reference F1 0.879; Another embedding model F1 0.762
